executive_summary: The ACCC (which also houses the Australian Energy Regulator) is an expertise-heavy economic regulator in the Treasury portfolio. The supplied corporate plan content is effectively a landing-page stub with no substantive body text, so this brief is built largely from external research and the ACCC's well-documented operating context rather than the plan itself. The dominant, time-bound demand signals are the new mandatory merger-notification regime commencing 1 January 2026, the funded supermarket/grocery enforcement program and price inquiry, the National Anti-Scam Centre, and repeated ANAO probity scrutiny. As a regulator, the best-fit opportunities lie in Strategy/Policy/Governance, Data & Analytics, Digital/ICT Delivery and Assurance/Probity rather than large transactional delivery programs.

strategic_priorities.title: Implementing the new merger control regime
strategic_priorities.description: From 1 January 2026 Australia moves to a mandatory, suspensory merger-notification system administered by the ACCC, requiring new processes, systems, case-handling capacity and economic analysis capability. This is the most significant structural change to the ACCC's functions in years.

strategic_priorities.title: Supermarket and retail competition focus
strategic_priorities.description: The ACCC received dedicated funding for supermarket/retail investigation and enforcement and was directed to run a 12-month grocery price inquiry, creating a funded, time-boxed program of market study and enforcement activity.

strategic_priorities.title: Scams disruption via the National Anti-Scam Centre
strategic_priorities.description: The ACCC hosts the NASC, requiring data-sharing, intelligence and analytics capability across banks, telcos and digital platforms.

strategic_priorities.title: Compliance and enforcement priorities
strategic_priorities.description: The ACCC sets annual compliance and enforcement priorities (e.g. misleading surcharging, consumer guarantees, digital platforms) and pursues major litigation such as ACCC v Qantas.

reform_and_change_drivers: Commencement of the mandatory merger-notification regime on 1 January 2026 — new legislative function requiring new processes and systems
$30m of dedicated supermarket/retail enforcement funding (over ~3.5 years) plus a government-directed 12-month grocery price inquiry
National Anti-Scam Centre operations requiring multi-party data sharing and analytics
High-profile, resource-intensive litigation (e.g. ACCC v Qantas 'ghost flights')
Repeated ANAO scrutiny of probity management within the ACCC
challenges_and_risks: Standing up an entirely new merger-review function (people, process, systems) to a hard legislative deadline
Surge resourcing and analytical capacity to deliver both the grocery inquiry and ongoing enforcement
Integrity/probity exposure given the ACCC's market-sensitive information and the ANAO's repeated probity focus
Demonstrating regulatory effectiveness and outcomes through credible performance measures
Data integration and intelligence quality across the anti-scam ecosystem

news_and_controversies.summary: The ACCC is pursuing Qantas over alleged sale of more than 8,000 tickets for already-cancelled 'ghost flights', seeking at least $250m in penalties, linked to the $570m travel-credit controversy.
news_and_controversies.implication: Major litigation creates demand for economic/evidence analysis, surge capacity and post-matter capability uplift — though this is the ACCC acting, not a failure of the agency.

news_and_controversies.summary: Government allocated $30m to the ACCC (over ~3.5 years from 2024) for dedicated supermarket/retail investigation and enforcement, and directed a 12-month grocery price inquiry.
news_and_controversies.implication: A funded, time-boxed program is a near-term procurement signal for market study, economic analysis, data and surge-capacity work.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit(s) on Probity Management in financial regulators and within the ACCC specifically. Detailed findings/recommendations were not retrievable in the research.
anao_findings.implication: Probity, conflict-of-interest and integrity-framework assurance is a live ANAO focus for the ACCC — a credible entry point for governance and assurance (non-legal probity) services.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit 'Managing Compliance with Fair Trading Obligations' examining the effectiveness of the ACCC's core compliance/enforcement administration. Detailed conclusions not retrievable in research.
anao_findings.implication: Audit attention on enforcement administration points to demand for program evaluation and process-improvement work.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Stand up a new mandatory merger-notification function (process, governance, case workflow) to a 1 January 2026 deadline
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Delivery/PMO support and independent implementation assurance for the new merger regime, including gateway-style health checks ahead of go-live
opportunity_areas.rationale: A legislated new function with a hard deadline is classic time-bound implementation demand; assurance reduces go-live risk for a high-profile regulatory change.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Digital intake, workflow and information-handling system to administer mandatory notifications
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and service design for a merger-notification portal, then build/integration of case-management workflow and secure information handling
opportunity_areas.rationale: A mandatory notification regime needs a public-facing intake and internal workflow system; the firm can both design and build. Inferred from the regime's operational requirements.

opportunity_areas.area: Grocery inquiry and supermarket enforcement analytics
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Market study, pricing analysis and intelligence to support a funded 12-month grocery inquiry and ongoing retail enforcement
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Data analytics, pricing/market analysis and research support to the inquiry team, plus intelligence tooling for the supermarket enforcement program
opportunity_areas.rationale: $30m of dedicated funding and a directed inquiry create a near-term, analytics-heavy workload. Confirmed by external research on the funding and inquiry.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Respond to ANAO probity scrutiny and strengthen conflict-of-interest controls around market-sensitive information
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent (non-legal) probity framework review and probity advisory, complemented by governance assurance against ANAO findings
opportunity_areas.rationale: Repeated ANAO probity audits of the ACCC signal sensitivity and likely remediation activity. Directly supported by ANAO research.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Integrate and analyse cross-sector data feeds for the National Anti-Scam Centre
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Information-strategy and data-management support, plus business intelligence to improve scam intelligence sharing across banks, telcos and platforms
opportunity_areas.rationale: NASC's effectiveness depends on multi-party data integration and analytics; a plausible but inferred need given thin plan detail.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Demonstrate outcomes from enforcement and regulatory activity through credible performance measures
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: PGPA-aligned performance-measure review and a process/outcome evaluation of a major compliance program (e.g. fair trading)
opportunity_areas.rationale: ANAO attention on fair-trading compliance administration suggests value in evaluation and performance-framework work for a regulator.

recommended_actions.action: Obtain and read the full ACCC & AER Corporate Plan 2025-26 PDF to confirm named priorities, risks and capability gaps before pitching
recommended_actions.rationale: The supplied text is a stub; the actual plan is essential to ground any offer and avoid generic pitches.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the merger-reform implementation team / relevant Executive General Manager with an implementation-assurance and PMO capability statement ahead of the 1 January 2026 commencement
recommended_actions.rationale: The hard legislative deadline makes this the most time-bound and credible opportunity.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short probity/governance assurance proposition referencing the ANAO probity audits for the General Counsel/Chief Operating Officer and audit & risk committee
recommended_actions.rationale: Repeated ANAO probity scrutiny is a verified, low-sensitivity entry point for assurance work.

recommended_actions.action: Pull the full ANAO reports (probity and fair-trading compliance) for ratings, recommendations and ACCC agreed responses, and re-run Parliamentary/Estimates searches on aph.gov.au
recommended_actions.rationale: Research was cut off before findings and Parliamentary material could be verified; closing these gaps sharpens the pitch.

engagement_targets: Executive General Manager / branch leading merger reform implementation
Chief Operating Officer and corporate/enabling services group
Chief Information Officer / digital and data functions
talking_points: The mandatory merger regime from 1 January 2026 is a fixed-deadline implementation challenge — we can provide delivery/PMO support and independent go-live assurance.
We can stand up or assure the digital intake and case-management capability the new notification regime requires — we build, not just advise.
ANAO's repeated probity focus on the ACCC creates a clear, low-sensitivity case for an independent probity-framework review (non-legal).
The funded grocery inquiry and supermarket enforcement program need surge analytics and market-study capacity we can supply quickly.
As a regulator, your performance story matters — we can refresh PGPA-aligned measures and evaluate a major compliance program.
caveats: The provided corporate plan text contained no substantive content — only publication metadata — so strategic priorities, risks and capability signals could not be drawn from the plan itself and are inferred from external research and the ACCC's known operating context. Opportunity confidence is therefore mostly Low/Medium and should not be relied upon until the full 2025-26 plan PDF is read. External research was also incomplete: ANAO findings/dates were not verified and no Parliamentary material was retrieved. Treat this brief as a starting hypothesis requiring confirmation.
